absolute_crystals.label: The Absolute — Keening Crystals / Growing Hollows
tavern: The mine foreman in [town] won't let anyone past the third level anymore. Says it's structural. Doesn't look structural.
My cousin works supply for the community center in Duren's Fold. Says they get twice the rations they hand out. Where's the rest going?
Something killed three goats on the Harlen farm. Not wolves. Whatever it was didn't eat them — just... stopped them.
There's a sound coming from under the old chapel at night. Low. Steady. Like something breathing that shouldn't be.
The Wardens cordoned off the Greymoss caves last month. Ecological survey, they said. Since when do Wardens care about ecology?
A merchant came through selling crystals he said he found near [site]. Beautiful things. His hands were shaking the whole time.
You know the sink hole on the east road? It's bigger. And it hums.
The community center in [town] has a new basement. Didn't used to have a basement.
They've been hiring construction crews from out of town. Local crews get turned away. Why?
Sister Aldine at the center says they're expanding the root cellar. Root cellar doesn't need reinforced stone walls.
The dogs won't go near the old resonance site anymore. Haven't for weeks.

Two kids went into the caves on a dare. One came back. Couldn't remember what happened to the other one. The other one came home an hour later, fine, but... flat. Like the light behind his eyes went out.
Something's wrong with the water near [site]. Tastes like metal. Tastes like sound, if that makes any sense.
notices: A community center with more security than it needs
Supply deliveries that don't match the output
Sounds at resonance sites that shouldn't be there — low, persistent, growing
Echo Sick symptoms in people living near sites (flat affect, repetitive speech, loss of interest)
Church personnel rotating through small towns who have no obvious reason to be there
Construction that doesn't match the stated purpose
Animals avoiding an area
Luke's beauty-sense registering wrongness at a site — things look uglier than they should

absolute_daggers.label: The Absolute — Boosting the Velvet Daggers / Glimpse
tavern: The Velvet Daggers got the Bellanthorpe residency. They're good, but they're not *that* good. Someone's spending money.
Did you hear? The Daggers have a new patron. Anonymous. Covering their travel, their lodging, everything.
Pip's been meeting with people who aren't in the music business. Suits. Church-adjacent types.
The Daggers played [town] last week. Full house. Half the audience was comped. Who pays for five hundred free tickets?
Blaid's been wearing new gear. Enchanted strings, if I had to guess. That's ten years' salary for a touring musician.
There's a rumor the Daggers are being lined up for a command performance in Valenmoor. For the High Conservator himself.
I saw the Daggers' rider for [venue]. They need a specific crystal array behind the stage. Not standard equipment. Nobody I asked had seen one like it.
Someone bought every copy of the Daggers' recording in [town]. Every single one. Who does that?
A friend of mine tunes instruments for touring acts. Says the Daggers' gear has been modified — not better, exactly, but different. Tuned to something he didn't recognize.
The Daggers used to play the small circuit. Same as everyone. Now they're headlining festivals they didn't apply to.
Pip dropped three names at the after-party. All Conservatory board. Since when does a band manager know Conservatory board members?
Tod — the drummer — looked uncomfortable after the show. Like he wanted to say something. Didn't.
notices: The Daggers appearing at every major venue the party plays, always slightly ahead
Production quality that exceeds what a touring band should afford
Pip's network expanding visibly — new contacts, new access, new confidence
Tod's discomfort growing (he's the honest one; he can feel something is off)
Equipment the Daggers are using that Barakas or AV would recognize as unusual or expensive
Audiences at Daggers shows that feel... managed. Too uniform in their response. Not fake, but curated.
Blayde playing differently — technically perfect but reaching for something beyond technique, as if being pushed toward transcendence she didn't choose

absolute_sera.label: The Absolute — Hunting Sera and ToneTum / Heartgifts
tavern: A woman matching Sera's description was seen in [town] three days ago. Two Church officers arrived asking about her the next day.
Someone's been asking about Greywood survivors in the old taverns. Offering coin for information. Not saying who they work for.
A halfling monk in the Eastern Highlands had visitors last month. Unusual — he doesn't get visitors.
The Church has started keeping records of bards over level 8. Performance licenses now require a full background filing.
An old elf woman played the Broken Drum in [town]. Packed house. She was gone by morning. Two days later, Wardens came asking for a description.
There's a bounty board in [town] that doesn't list crimes. Just names and 'wanted for questioning.' Sera Voss is on it.
A merchant on the eastern road says Church scouts are mapping hermitage sites in the Highlands. Every monastery, every retreat, every cave with a door on it.
Someone broke into the Thornwick municipal archive. Nothing taken — but the Greywood section was thoroughly read. Pages left out of order.
I heard the Church is offering pensions to retired adventurers who served at Greywood. Generous ones. All they have to do is come in for an interview.
Sera Voss hasn't played a public show in months. For a bard of her reputation, that's not retirement. That's hiding.
A friend in [town] says someone approached him about his sister — she used to travel with a Resonant cell. They wanted to know who her friends were. Specifically, who she'd die for.
There are new postings at the Warden stations: 'Persons of cultural significance — report sightings.' No names. Just descriptions.
notices: Warden interest in bards and musicians that goes beyond licensing
Questions about Greywood that feel pointed — not historical curiosity, but operational intelligence gathering
Church officers in places they wouldn't normally be, asking about people who match Sera's or ToneTum's descriptions
Rick's Sera connection ("find me in Valenmoor") becoming a thread someone else is also pulling
If the party mentions Sera by name in the wrong place, increased attention follows
Background checks on the party themselves — someone is mapping their relationships

absolute_antires.label: The Absolute — Anti-Resonant Operations
tavern: Resonants bombed a grain store in [town]. Thirty families lost their winter supply.
The Wardens found Resonant pamphlets in a dead man's house. He was a schoolteacher.
I used to think the Resonants had a point. Then they burned the [building]. Lost my sympathy.
A Resonant cell got caught in [town]. The Wardens had their names, their meeting place, their schedule. Someone talked.
The Church is offering amnesty to anyone who turns in Resonant contacts. No questions asked. Full pardon.
Supply caravans to the western reaches are being hit. The Church says it's Resonants. The drivers say the attackers wore Church boots.
My neighbor reported a Resonant. Got a month's rations as a reward. We don't talk anymore.
The Wardens arrested a Pale Hand priest last week. Called him a Resonant sympathizer. He was eighty years old.
A broadsheet in [town] is running stories about Resonant violence. Three stories this week. All sourced to 'Church security officials.' No witnesses named.
Someone painted the broken string on the town hall. Next morning, three shops had their windows smashed. The broadsheet blamed the Resonants. The shopkeepers said it was men in uniform.
There's a new Resonant pamphlet going around — except it's calling for armed uprising. The real Resonants say it's not theirs.
The Church announced a 'Community Safety Fund.' Volunteers get a stipend, a badge, and a list of names to watch.
A Resonant safe house in [town] was raided. The family hiding them was publicly named. They've been shunned.
I heard the Resonants poisoned a well in [town].
notices: Stories about Resonant violence that don't add up when you ask follow-up questions
Evidence at "Resonant attack" sites that looks staged (Church equipment, too-clean operational patterns)
Genuine Resonant cells going quieter — harder to find, more paranoid, less willing to trust new contacts
Marta's exposure risk increasing as Breck's capture is exploited for intelligence
Public opinion shifting against the Resonants in towns the party revisits
Resonant contacts the party has met being harder to reach or having gone dark
The gap between what the broadsheets say and what the party has seen with their own eyes

absolute_maelon.label: The Absolute — Attacking the Church of Maelon
tavern: The Conservatory opened a hospice in [town]. Nice place, actually. The Pale Hand priest looked... lost.
My mother died last week. The Kethara priest did the rites. Felt wrong, but the Pale Hand priest was gone. Nobody knew where.
The Pale Hand temple in [town] lost its funding. Operating on donations now. The Conservatory temple across the street just got a new roof.
A Conservatory broadsheet says Maelon's followers have 'failed to meet the spiritual needs of the bereaved.' Harsh.
The Pale Hand hospice I used to volunteer at closed. The new Kethara one opened the same week. Same building.
A Pale Hand priest was refused entry to the dying ward at [town] hospital. New policy. Conservatory clergy only.
My grandfather wants a Maelon funeral. The family is arguing about whether it's worth the paperwork.
There's a Conservatory pamphlet going around: 'In your hour of need, trust the proven faith.' It's got Kethara's seal. No mention of Maelon.
The old death-singer in [town] was told to apply for a Conservatory license if she wants to keep practicing. She's been singing the dead home for forty years.
I heard Maelon's temples are losing their tax exemptions. Something about 'failure to meet community standards.'
A Pale Hand priest in [town] was arrested for performing unauthorized funeral rites. Unauthorized. For a funeral.
The Conservatory is training its own end-of-life clergy. Six-week course. The Pale Hand apprenticeship takes twelve years.
notices: Pale Hand clergy being displaced from their traditional roles — visibly confused, hurt, compliant
Kethara hospices that are competent but missing something — the rites feel hollow, procedural
Rick recognizing old Pale Hand equipment in Conservatory hands, rebranded
Frederick's world shrinking — his institutional support eroding
Gnomebare and other Pale Hand contacts becoming more isolated, more desperate
The emotional texture of death changing in towns — grief without release, funerals without rest
Towns where the party played earlier feeling different on return — something intangible gone from the death rites

chadwick.label: Chadwick's Withdrawal
tavern: The High Conservator cancelled his appearance at the [festival/ceremony]. Third time this season.
I heard Chadwick is ill. No — I heard he's fine and just busy. No — I heard he hasn't been seen in weeks.
A friend at the Conservatory says the inner circle is tighter than ever. Nobody gets in without three levels of clearance.
There was supposed to be a public blessing at [event]. A deputy showed up instead. Read from a script.
Chadwick's last public address was... strange. He repeated a phrase. Then corrected himself. Then repeated it again.
The Church says he's focused on 'deep spiritual work.' That's what they said about the last Grand Chancellor before he died.
Someone tried to get an audience with the High Conservator through the official channels. Got a letter back. Signed by someone they'd never heard of.
Security around the Solmara compound has tripled. The Church says it's because of Resonant threats.
I saw a painting of Chadwick from ten years ago. He looked like a different person. Not older — different.
A Conservatory officer I know says Chadwick's orders have been... inconsistent. One day hawkish, the next almost gentle. Then hawkish again, harder.
The deputies are running things now. Everyone knows it. Nobody says it.
notices: Official Church communications that feel like they come from a committee, not a person
Deputies and intermediaries where there should be direct contact
If the party gets close to Chadwick: moments of lucidity that are startling and brief — a different person behind the eyes
Security escalation framed as protection that functions as containment
Other high-status musicians and cultural figures also noting the difficulty of access

resonants.label: Resonant Activity
tavern: Someone played a banned song in the market square in [town]. Full crowd. Wardens showed up but the player was gone. People were humming it for days.
A measurement station on the north road was smashed overnight. Clean job. No guards hurt.
The Pale Hand priest in [town] was supposed to be evicted. Someone hid him. Nobody's talking about who.
A Resonant pamphlet turned up in my bread delivery. Wrapped around the loaf. It had numbers — site readings that don't match what the Church says.
There was a sit-in at the Warden office in [town]. Twelve people. Wouldn't leave until they got answers about the crop failures. The Wardens waited them out.
A supply caravan carrying Conservatory measurement devices was hit on the [road]. Devices smashed. Drivers untouched. Left them water and an apology note.
My daughter found a broken-string symbol carved into a tree on the east road. Fresh.
Someone smuggled a pre-Silence measurement device into [town]. A merchant was offering readings for a copper. The Church shut him down in hours.
The Resonants are getting bolder. Or more desperate. Hard to tell which.
An old woman in [town] played Maelon's rest-hymn at a funeral. Out loud. In public. Nobody stopped her. Nobody reported her, either.
I heard the Resonants have a printing press somewhere in the western reaches. Pamphlets showing up faster than the Church can burn them.
notices: Broken-string symbols in unexpected places — carved into furniture, scratched on walls, tattooed on wrists
Music that shouldn't be playing — old songs, banned songs, songs that feel like they come from before
Ordinary people doing small acts of resistance without knowing they're part of anything
The Resonant network being harder to access as the Absolute's operations tighten
Mave and Oisin's cell being more cautious after Thornwall
Marta's anxiety increasing — Breck's capture is an ongoing threat to her entire network

the_label.label: The Label
tavern: A bard in [town] signed a contract with a patron nobody's met. Career took off overnight. She doesn't talk about the terms.
There's a manager working the festival circuit who always seems to know which acts will break through. Like he's already seen it.
A recording crystal from a show in [town] sold for a hundred gold. The performance wasn't that good. But something about it...
My friend played the best show of his life last month. Said it felt like something was listening. Not the audience — something behind the audience.
There are new contracts circulating for touring musicians. Standard terms, nothing unusual on paper. But the musicians who sign them play... differently. More driven. Less happy.
A patron in [town] is buying up performance venues. Cash. No negotiation. Doesn't seem to care about profit.
Barakas got an offer he didn't tell the band about.
The Label isn't a person. It's not a company. It's a... feeling. Like the room is watching you and deciding whether you're worth its time.
notices: Barakas receiving nudges, opportunities, or information he shouldn't have — and not always questioning where it comes from
Performances that feel observed by something beyond the audience
Other musicians on the circuit with similar "too-fast" career trajectories and similar unnamed patrons
The sense that the music industry has an invisible hand that isn't the Church
Contracts and deals that benefit the party arriving at suspiciously convenient moments
A feeling after transcendent performance moments — something taken, not just given

general.label: General World Deterioration
tavern: The harvest in [region] came in at half. Second year running.
The fish aren't running in [river]. First time in living memory.
A whole field of wheat grew in silence. No birdsong, no wind noise, nothing. Farmer says it grew twice as fast and tasted like nothing.
The mine in [town] hit a vein that sings. Not metaphorically. The ore hums. Miners won't go back.
Weather's wrong. It shouldn't be this warm / cold / dry / wet for the season.
The stars looked different last night. My grandmother says they've shifted. Probably just her eyes.
There are more beggars on the road this year. Towns that used to be generous are closing their gates.
A caravan from [town] arrived with half its goods spoiled. Not rotted — just... empty. Like the substance went out of them.
The old songs don't work as well. My mother used to sing to the garden and everything grew. I sing the same songs. Nothing.
A resonance site near [town] has been humming louder. You can hear it from the road now. Used to be you had to press your ear to the stone.
Three towns on the [road] have posted guards at their granaries. Never used to need them.
The river's running slower. Not lower — slower. Like it's tired.
An instrument maker in [town] says her wood is different. Same supplier, same forest. But the tone is wrong. Flat. Dead.
People are angrier. I don't mean politics. I mean — the baker yelled at a child yesterday. He's never yelled at anyone.
The Church says the program is working. The fields say otherwise.
Something's wrong with the silence between notes. It used to feel like rest. Now it feels like something holding its breath.
notices: Music that felt vibrant in early sessions feeling slightly harder to play, slightly less responsive
NPCs they met earlier showing subtle changes — shorter tempers, flatter affect, less joy
Prices rising in towns they revisit
Roads less maintained, fewer travelers, more tension at checkpoints
Resonance sites the party has visited feeling different — louder, more pressured, harder to be near
Duane feeling the overfullness in his bones before anyone else names it
Luke seeing increasing ugliness — not dramatic, just a steady erosion of beauty in the world
AV's technical knowledge registering that instruments and acoustic equipment are behaving differently
Rick recognizing Maelon's absence in the quality of death — funerals that don't release, grief that doesn't process
